Equivalent (JA): 解析ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Analysis Domain Library provides library models supporting the modeling of analysis cases (see 7.23 ) and related analysis tasks.
Context/Example (EN): The Analysis Domain Library provides library models supporting the modeling of analysis cases (see 7.23 ) and related analysis tasks.

Definition (EN): ToolExecution metadata identifies an external analysis tool to be used to implement the annotated action.
Context/Example (EN): ToolExecution metadata identifies an external analysis tool to be used to implement the annotated action.

Definition (EN): ToolVariable metadata is used in the context of an action that has been annotated with ToolExecution metadata. It is used to annotate a parameter or other feature of the action with the name of the variable in the tool that is to correspond to the annotated feature.
Context/Example (EN): ToolVariable metadata is used in the context of an action that has been annotated with ToolExecution metadata.

Definition (EN): SampledFunction is a variable-size, ordered collection of SamplePair elements that represents a generic, discretely sampled, uni-variate or multi-variate mathematical function. The function must be montonic, either strictly increasing or strictly decreasing.
Context/Example (EN): A SampledFunction can be used for many engineering purposes. For example, it can represent a time series observation, where the domain consists of time instants expressed on a given timescale and the range consists of observed quantity values.

Definition (EN): Domain returns the sequence of the domainValues of all samples in a SampledFunction.
Context/Example (EN): Domain returns the sequence of the domainValues of all samples in a SampledFunction.

Definition (EN): An Interpolate calculation returns an interpolated range value from a given SampledFunction for a given domain value. If the input domain value is outside the bounds of the domainValues of the SampleFunction, null is returned.
Context/Example (EN): An Interpolate calculation returns an interpolated range value from a given SampledFunction for a given domain value.

Definition (EN): interpolateLinear is an Interpolate calculation assuming a linear functional form between SamplePairs.
Context/Example (EN): interpolateLinear is an Interpolate calculation assuming a linear functional form between SamplePairs.

Definition (EN): Range returns the sequence of the rangeValues of all samples in a SampledFunction.
Context/Example (EN): Range returns the sequence of the rangeValues of all samples in a SampledFunction.

Definition (EN): Sample returns a SampledFunction that samples a given calculation over a sequence of domainValues.
Context/Example (EN): Sample returns a SampledFunction that samples a given calculation over a sequence of domainValues.

Definition (EN): SamplePair is a key-value pair of a domainValue and a rangeValue, used as a sample element in SampledFunction.
Context/Example (EN): SamplePair is a key-value pair of a domainValue and a rangeValue, used as a sample element in SampledFunction.

Definition (EN): State Space Representation (SSR) is a foundational dynamical systems representation, commonly used in control systems.
Context/Example (EN): State Space Representation (SSR) is a foundational dynamical systems representation, commonly used in control systems.

Definition (EN): A TradeStudy is an analysis case whose subject is a set of alternatives (at least one) and whose result is a selection of one of those alternatives. The alternatives are evaluated based on a given ObjectiveFunction and the selection is made such that it satisfies the objective of the TradeStudy (which must be a TradeStudyObjective).
Context/Example (EN): A trade-off analysis is a special kind of analysis used to evaluate and compare alternatives. Such an analysis can be modeled by a usage of the TradeStudy analysis case definition from the TradeStudies library model found in the Analysis Domain Library (see 9.4.5 ).

Definition (EN): An EvaluationFunction is a calculation that evaluates a TradeStudy alternative, producing a ScalarValue that can be compared with the evaluation of other alternatives.
Context/Example (EN): An EvaluationFunction is a calculation that evaluates a TradeStudy alternative, producing a ScalarValue that can be compared with the evaluation of other alternatives.

Definition (EN): A MaximizeObjective is a TradeStudyObjective that requires that the selectedAlternative have the maximum EvaluationFunction value of all the given alternatives.
Context/Example (EN): A MaximizeObjective is a TradeStudyObjective that requires that the selectedAlternative have the maximum EvaluationFunction value of all the given alternatives.

Definition (EN): A MinimizeObjective is a TradeStudyObjective that requires that the selectedAlternative have the minimum EvaluationFunction value of all the given alternatives.
Context/Example (EN): A MinimizeObjective is a TradeStudyObjective that requires that the selectedAlternative have the minimum EvaluationFunction value of all the given alternatives.

Equivalent (JA): トレードオフ分析目標
Definition (EN): A TradeStudyObjective is the base definition for the objective of a TradeStudy. The requirement is to choose from a given set of alternatives the selectedAlternative for that has the best evaluation according to a given EvaluationFunction. What value is considered "best" is not defined in the abstract base definition but must be computed in any concrete specialization.
Context/Example (EN): A TradeStudyObjective is the base definition for the objective of a TradeStudy.

Equivalent (JA): 因果関係ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Cause and Effect Domain Library contains a library model of cause and effect relationships and metadata supporting its use.
Context/Example (EN): The Cause and Effect Domain Library contains a library model of cause and effect relationships and metadata supporting its use.

Definition (EN): A Causation is a binary Multicausation in which a single cause occurrence causes a single effect occurrence. (However, a single cause can separately have multiple effects, and a single effect can have separate Causation connections with multiple causes.)
Context/Example (EN): A Causation is a binary Multicausation in which a single cause occurrence causes a single effect occurrence.

Definition (EN): causations is the base feature for Causation ConnectionUsages.
Context/Example (EN): causations is the base feature for Causation ConnectionUsages.

Definition (EN): Occurrences that are causes.
Context/Example (EN): The causing Occurrences. (Constant for each Multicausation instance.) Redefines CausationConnections::causes in the context of Multicausation.

Definition (EN): Occurrences that are effects.
Context/Example (EN): The effect Occurrences caused by the causing Occurrences. (Constant for each Multicausation instance.) Redefines CausationConnections::effects in the context of Multicausation.

Definition (EN): A Multicausation connection models the situation in which one set of occurrences causes another. All causes must at least exist before all effects.
Context/Example (EN): A Multicausation connection models the situation in which one set of occurrences causes another.

Definition (EN): multicausations is the base feature for Multicausation ConnectionUsages.
Context/Example (EN): multicausations is the base feature for Multicausation ConnectionUsages.

Definition (EN): CausationMetadata allows for the specification of additional metadata about a cause-effect ConnectionDefinition or ConnectionUsage.
Context/Example (EN): CausationMetadata allows for the specification of additional metadata about a cause-effect ConnectionDefinition or ConnectionUsage.

Equivalent (JA): 因果関係セマンティックメタデータ
Definition (EN): CausationMetadata (short name causation) is SemanticMetadata for a Causation connection.
Context/Example (EN): CausationMetadata (short name causation) is SemanticMetadata for a Causation connection.

Definition (EN): CauseMetadata (short name cause) identifies a Usage as being a cause occurrence. It is intended to be used to tag the cause ends of a Multicausation.
Context/Example (EN): CauseMetadata (short name cause) identifies a Usage as being a cause occurrence.

Definition (EN): EffectMetadata (short name effect) identifies a Usage as being a effect occurrence. It is intended to be used to tag the effect ends of a Multicausation.
Context/Example (EN): EffectMetadata (short name effect) identifies a Usage as being a effect occurrence.

Equivalent (JA): 多重因果関係セマンティックメタデータ
Definition (EN): MulticausationMetadata (short name multicausation) is SemanticMetadata for a Multicausation connection.
Context/Example (EN): MulticausationMetadata (short name multicausation) is SemanticMetadata for a Multicausation connection.

Equivalent (JA): 要求導出ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Requirement Derivation Domain Library contains a library model of requirement derivation relationships and metadata supporting its use.
Context/Example (EN): The Requirement Derivation Domain Library contains a library model of requirement derivation relationships and metadata supporting its use.

Definition (EN): A Derivation connection asserts that one or more derivedRequirements are derived from a single originalRequirement. This means that any subject that satisfies the originalRequirement should, in itself or though other things related to it, satisfy each of the derivedRequirements.
Context/Example (EN): A Derivation connection asserts that one or more derivedRequirements are derived from a single originalRequirement.

Definition (EN): derivations is the base feature for Derivation ConnectionUsages.
Context/Example (EN): derivations is the base feature for Derivation ConnectionUsages.

Definition (EN): derivedRequirements are the derived requirements in Derivation connections.
Context/Example (EN): derivedRequirements are the derived requirements in Derivation connections.

Definition (EN): originalRequirements are the original requirements in Derivation connections.
Context/Example (EN): originalRequirements are the original requirements in Derivation connections.

Definition (EN): DerivationMetadata (short name derivation) is SemanticMetadata for a Derivation connection.
Context/Example (EN): DerivationMetadata (short name derivation) is SemanticMetadata for a Derivation connection.

Definition (EN): DerivedlRequirementMetadata (short name derive) identifies a Usage as a derived requirement. It is intended to be used to tag the derived requirement ends of a Derivation.
Context/Example (EN): DerivedlRequirementMetadata (short name derive) identifies a Usage as a derived requirement.

Definition (EN): OriginalRequirementMetadata (short name original) identifies a Usage as an original requirement. It is intended to be used to tag the original requirement end of a Derivation.
Context/Example (EN): OriginalRequirementMetadata (short name original) identifies a Usage as an original requirement.

Equivalent (JA): 幾何ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Geometry Domain Library contains a general library model of physical items with spatial extent and a library model of specific spatial items that represent basic geometric shapes.
Context/Example (EN): The Geometry Domain Library contains a general library model of physical items with spatial extent and a library model of specific spatial items that represent basic geometric shapes.

Definition (EN): The CurrentDisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the DisplacementOf the Points relative to the SpacialItem, at the currentTime of the Clock.
Context/Example (EN): The CurrentDisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the DisplacementOf the Points relative to the SpacialItem, at the currentTime of the Clock.

Definition (EN): The CurrentPositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the PositionOf the Point relative to the SpatialItem at the currentTime of the Clock.
Context/Example (EN): The CurrentPositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the PositionOf the Point relative to the SpatialItem at the currentTime of the Clock.

Definition (EN): The DisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, is the displacementVector computed as the difference between the PositionOf the first Point and PositionOf the second Point, relative to that SpatialItem, at that timeInstant
Context/Example (EN): The DisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, is the displacementVector computed as the difference between the PositionOf the first Point and PositionOf the second Point, relative to that SpatialItem, at that timeInstant

Definition (EN): The PositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, as a positionVector that is a VectorQuantityValue in the coordinateFrame of the SpatialItem. The default Clock is the localClock of the SpatialItem.
Context/Example (EN): The PositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, as a positionVector that is a VectorQuantityValue in the coordinateFrame of the SpatialItem.

Definition (EN): A SpatialItem is an Item with a three-dimensional spatial extent that also acts as a SpatialFrame of reference.
Context/Example (EN): A SpatialItem is an Item with a three-dimensional spatial extent that also acts as a SpatialFrame of reference.

Definition (EN): A Circle is an Ellipse with semiaxes equal to its radius.
Context/Example (EN): A Circle is an Ellipse with semiaxes equal to its radius.

Definition (EN): A CircularCone is a Cone with a circular base.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularCone is a Cone with a circular base.

Definition (EN): A CircularCylinder is a Cylinder with two circular sides.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularCylinder is a Cylinder with two circular sides.

Definition (EN): A CircularDisc is a Disc bound by a Circle.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularDisc is a Disc bound by a Circle.

Definition (EN): A Cone has one elliptical sides joined to a point by a curved side.
Context/Example (EN): A Cone has one elliptical sides joined to a point by a curved side.

Definition (EN): A ConeOrCylinder is a Cone or a Cylinder with a given elliptical base, height, width (perpendicular distance from the base to the center of the top side or vertex), and offsets of this perpendicular at the base from the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A ConeOrCylinder is a Cone or a Cylinder with a given elliptical base, height, width (perpindicular distance from the base to the center of the top side or vertex), and offsets of this perpindicular at the base from the center of the base.

Definition (EN): A ConicSection is a closed PlanarCurve, possibly disconnected, see Hyperbola.
Context/Example (EN): A ConicSection is a closed PlanarCurve, possibly disconnected, see Hyperbola.

Definition (EN): A ConicSurface is a Surface that has ConicSection cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A ConicSurface is a Surface that has ConicSection cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Cuboid is a Polyhedron with six sides, all quadrilateral.
Context/Example (EN): A Cuboid is a Polyhedron with six sides, all quadrilateral.

Definition (EN): A CuboidOrTriangularPrism is a Polyhedron that is either a Cuboid or TriangularPrism.
Context/Example (EN): A CuboidOrTriangularPrism is a Polyhedron that is either a Cuboid or TriangularPrism.

Definition (EN): A Cylinder has two elliptical sides joined by a curved side.
Context/Example (EN): A Cylinder has two elliptical sides joined by a curved side.

Definition (EN): A Disc is a Shell bound by an Ellipse.
Context/Example (EN): A Disc is a Shell bound by an Ellipse.

Definition (EN): An EccentricCone is a Cone with least one positive offset.
Context/Example (EN): An EccentricCone is a Cone with least one positive offset.

Definition (EN): An EccentricCylinder is a Cylinder
Context/Example (EN): An EccentricCylinder is a Cylinder

Definition (EN): An Ellipse is a ConicSection in the shape of an ellipse of given semiaxes.
Context/Example (EN): An Ellipse is a ConicSection in the shape of an ellipse of given semiaxes.

Definition (EN): An Ellipsoid is a ConicSurface with only elliptical cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): An Ellipsoid is a ConicSurface with only elliptical cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Hyperbola is a planar Path in the shape of a hyperbola with given axes.
Context/Example (EN): A Hyperbola is a planar Path in the shape of a hyperbola with given axes.

Definition (EN): A Hyperboloid is a ConicSurface with only hyperbolic cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A Hyperboloid is a ConicSurface with only hyperbolic cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Line is a PlanarCurve that is straight.
Context/Example (EN): A Line is a PlanarCurve that is straight.

Definition (EN): A Parabola is a planar Path in the shape of a parabola of a given focal length.
Context/Example (EN): A Parabola is a planar Path in the shape of a parabola of a given focal length.

Definition (EN): A Paraboloid is a ConicSurface with only parabolic cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A Paraboloid is a ConicSurface with only parabolic cross-sections.

Definition (EN): Path is the most general structured Curve.
Context/Example (EN): Path is the most general structured Curve.

Definition (EN): A PlanarCurve is a Curve with a given length embeddable in a plane.
Context/Example (EN): A PlanarCurve is a Curve with a given length embeddable in a plane.

Definition (EN): A PlanarSurface is a Surface with given area that is flat.
Context/Example (EN): A PlanarSurface is a Surface with given area that is flat.

Definition (EN): A Polygon is a closed planar Path with straight edges.
Context/Example (EN): A Polygon is a closed planar Path with straight edges.

Definition (EN): A Polyhedron is a closed Shell with polygonal sides.
Context/Example (EN): A Polyhedron is a closed Shell with polygonal sides.

Definition (EN): A Pyramid is a Polyhedron with the sides of a polygon (base) forming the bases of triangles that join at an apex point. Its height is the perpindicular distance from the base to the apex, and its offsets are between this perpindicular at the base and the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A Pyramid is a Polyhedron with the sides of a polygon (base) forming the bases of triangles that join at an apex point.

Definition (EN): A Quadrilateral is a four-sided Polygon.
Context/Example (EN): A Quadrilateral is a four-sided Polygon.

Definition (EN): A Rectangle is a Quadrilateral with four right angles and given length and width.
Context/Example (EN): A Rectangle is a Quadrilateral with four right angles and given length and width.

Definition (EN): A RectangularCuboid is a Cuboid with all Rectangular sides.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularCuboid is a Cuboid with all Rectangular sides.

Definition (EN): A RectangularPyramid is a Pyramid with a rectangular base.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularPyramid is a Pyramid with a rectangular base.

Definition (EN): A RectangularToroid is a revolution of a Rectangle.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularToroid is a revolution of a Rectangle.

Definition (EN): A RightCircularCone is a CircularCone with zero offsets.
Context/Example (EN): A RightCircularCone is a CircularCone with zero offsets.

Definition (EN): A RightCircularCylinder is a CircularCylinder with zero offsets.
Context/Example (EN): A RightCircularCylinder is a CircularCylinder with zero offsets.

Definition (EN): A RightTriangle is a Triangle with edges opposite the hypotenuse at right angles.
Context/Example (EN): A RightTriangle is a Triangle with edges opposite the hypotenuse at right angles.

Definition (EN): A RightTriangularPrism is a TriangularPrism with two right triangular sides, with given length, width, and height.
Context/Example (EN): A RightTriangularPrism is a TriangularPrism with two right triangular sides, with given length, width, and height.

Definition (EN): Shell is the most general structured Surface.
Context/Example (EN): Shell is the most general structured Surface.

Definition (EN): A Sphere is an Ellipsoid with all the same semiaxes.
Context/Example (EN): A Sphere is an Ellipsoid with all the same semiaxes.

Definition (EN): A Tetrahedron is Pyramid with a triangular base.
Context/Example (EN): A Tetrahedron is Pyramid with a triangular base.

Definition (EN): A Toroid is a surface generated from revolving a planar closed curve about an line coplanar with the curve. It is single sided with one hole.
Context/Example (EN): A Toroid is a surface generated from revolving a planar closed curve about an line coplanar with the curve.

Definition (EN): A Torus is a revolution of a Circle.
Context/Example (EN): A Torus is a revolution of a Circle.

Definition (EN): A Triangle is three-sided Polygon with given length (base), width (perpindicular distance from base to apex), and offset of this perpindicular at the base from the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A Triangle is three-sided Polygon with given length (base), width (perpindicular distance from base to apex), and offset of this perpindicular at the base from the center of the base.

Definition (EN): A TriangularPrism is a Polyhedron with five sides, two triangular and the others quadrilateral.
Context/Example (EN): A TriangularPrism is a Polyhedron with five sides, two triangular and the others quadrilateral.

Equivalent (JA): 数量と単位ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): For any system model, a solid foundation for the representation of physical quantities, their units, scales, and quantity dimensions, as well as coordinate frames is essential.
Context/Example (EN): The Quantities and Units Domain Library defines reusable and extensible model elements for physical quantities, including vector and tensor quantities, quantity dimensions, measurement units, measurement scales, coordinate frames, coordinate transformations and vector spaces.

Definition (EN): Most general representation of real 3-vector quantities.
Context/Example (EN): Most general representation of real 3-vector quantities.

Definition (EN): A QuantityDimension is the product of powers of the set of base quantities defined for a particular system of quantities, units and scales.
Context/Example (EN): A QuantityDimension is the product of powers of the set of base quantities defined for a particular system of quantities, units and scales.

Definition (EN): A QuantityPowerFactor is a representation of a quantity power factor, being the combination of a quantity and an exponent.
Context/Example (EN): A QuantityPowerFactor is a representation of a quantity power factor, being the combination of a quantity and an exponent.

Definition (EN): AttributeUsage scalarQuantities : ScalarQuantityValue[*] nonunique is the subset of vectorQuantities that defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify scalar quantities of Occurrences.
Context/Example (EN): AttributeUsage scalarQuantities : ScalarQuantityValue[*] nonunique is the subset of vectorQuantities that defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify scalar quantities of Occurrences.

Definition (EN): A ScalarQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition that specializes VectorQuantityValue. It represents a scalar quantity value as a tuple of a Number num and a ScalarMeasurementReference mRef. By definition it has order zero.
Context/Example (EN): A ScalarQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition that specializes VectorQuantityValue.

Definition (EN): A SystemOfQuantities represents the essentials of [VIM] concept "system of quantities" (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.3.html), defined as a "set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities".
Context/Example (EN): A SystemOfQuantities represents the essentials of [VIM] concept "system of quantities" (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.3.html), defined as a "set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities".

Definition (EN): AttributeUsage tensorQuantities : TensorQuantityValue[*] nonunique defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify quantities of Occurrences.
Context/Example (EN): AttributeUsage tensorQuantities : TensorQuantityValue[*] nonunique defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify quantities of Occurrences.

Definition (EN): A TensorQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array that represents a tensor quantity value as a sequence of Numbers and a TensorMeasurementReference.
Context/Example (EN): A TensorQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array that represents a tensor quantity value as a sequence of Numbers and a TensorMeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): AttributeUsage vectorQuantities : VectorQuantityValue[*] nonunique is the subset of tensorQuantities that defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify vector quantities of Occurrences.
Context/Example (EN): AttributeUsage vectorQuantities : VectorQuantityValue[*] nonunique is the subset of tensorQuantities that defines a top-level general self-standing attribute that can be used to consistently specify vector quantities of Occurrences.

Definition (EN): A VectorQuantityValue is an AttributeDefinition that represents the value of a vector quantity by a tuple of Numbers and a VectorMeasurementReference. It is a specialization of TensorQuantityValue and has order one.
Context/Example (EN): A VectorQuantityValue is an AttributeDefinition that represents the value of a vector quantity by a tuple of Numbers and a VectorMeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): This package defines the general AttributeDefinitions and AttributeUsages to construct measurement references.
Context/Example (EN): This package defines the general AttributeDefinitions and AttributeUsages to construct measurement references.

Definition (EN): (No explicit description, but implied by context as a specific type of CoordinateFrame for 3D spatial coordinates.)
Context/Example (EN): General Types: CoordinateFrame. Features: dimensions : Positive [0..1] {redefines dimensions}.

Definition (EN): AffineTransformationMatrix3d is a three dimensional CoordinateTransformation specified via an affine 4x4 transformation matrix
Context/Example (EN): AffineTransformationMatrix3d is a three dimensional CoordinateTransformation specified via an affine 4x4 transformation matrix.

Definition (EN): ConversionByConvention is a UnitConversion that is defined according to some convention.
Context/Example (EN): ConversionByConvention is a UnitConversion that is defined according to some convention.

Definition (EN): ConversionByPrefix is a UnitConversion that is defined through reference to a named [ISO/IEC 80000-1] UnitPrefix, which represents a conversion factor that is a decimal or binary multiple or sub-multiple.
Context/Example (EN): ConversionByPrefix is a UnitConversion that is defined through reference to a named [ISO/IEC 80000-1] UnitPrefix, which represents a conversion factor that is a decimal or binary multiple or sub-multiple.

Definition (EN): A CoordinateFrame is a VectorMeasurementReference with the specific purpose to quantify (i.e., coordinatize) a vector space, and optionally locate and orient it with respect to another CoordinateFrame.
Context/Example (EN): A CoordinateFrame is a VectorMeasurementReference with the specific purpose to quantify (i.e., coordinatize) a vector space, and optionally locate and orient it with respect to another CoordinateFrame.

Definition (EN): CoordinateFramePlacement is a CoordinateTransformation by placement of the target frame in the source frame. Attribute origin specifies the location of the origin of the target frame through a vector in the source frame. Attribute basisDirections specifies the orientation of the target frame by specifying the directions of the respective basis vectors of the target frame via direction vectors in the source frame.
Context/Example (EN): CoordinateFramePlacement is a CoordinateTransformation by placement of the target frame in the source frame.

Definition (EN): A CoordinateTransformation is an AttributeDefinition that defines the transformation relationship between two coordinate systems, that are represented by VectorMeasurementReferences, typically CoordinateFrames.
Context/Example (EN): A CoordinateTransformation is an AttributeDefinition that defines the transformation relationship between two coordinate systems, that are represented by VectorMeasurementReferences, typically CoordinateFrames.

Definition (EN): (No explicit description, but implied as a feature for CountValue)
Context/Example (EN): General Types: dimensionOneQuantities, CountValue. Features: None.

Definition (EN): CountValue is an explicit definition of a generic "count" quantity as a DimensionOneValue.
Context/Example (EN): CountValue is an explicit definition of a generic "count" quantity as a DimensionOneValue.

Definition (EN): CyclicRationScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a ratio scale with a periodic cycle.
Context/Example (EN): CyclicRationScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a ratio scale with a periodic cycle. Example 1: "cyclic degree" (to express planar angular measures) with modulus 360 and unit 'degree'.

Definition (EN): A DefinitionalQuantityValue is a representation of a particular quantity value that is used in the definition of a MeasurementReference. Typically such a particular value is defined by convention. It can be used to define a selected reference value, such as the meaning of zero on a measurement scale or the origin of a top-level coordinate system.
Context/Example (EN): A DefinitionalQuantityValue is a representation of a particular quantity value that is used in the definition of a MeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): DerivedUnit is a MeasurementUnit that represents a measurement unit that depends on one or more powers of other measurement units.
Context/Example (EN): DerivedUnit is a MeasurementUnit that represents a measurement unit that depends on one or more powers of other measurement units.

Definition (EN): (No explicit description, but implied as a feature for DimensionOneValue)
Context/Example (EN): General Types: DimensionOneValue, scalarQuantities. Features: None.

Definition (EN): DimensionOneUnit is an explicit definition of "unit of dimension one", also known as "dimensionless unit".
Context/Example (EN): DimensionOneUnit is an explicit definition of "unit of dimension one", also known as "dimensionless unit".

Definition (EN): A DimensionOneValue is a ScalarQuantityValue with a DimensionOneUnit.
Context/Example (EN): A DimensionOneValue is a ScalarQuantityValue with a DimensionOneUnit.

Definition (EN): IntervalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a linear interval measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only intervals between two values are meaningful and not their ratios.
Context/Example (EN): IntervalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a linear interval measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only intervals between two values are meaningful and not their ratios.

Definition (EN): LogarithmicScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a logarithmic measurement scale that is defined as follows. The numeric value v of a ratio quantity expressed on a logarithmic scale equivalent with a value x of the same quantity expressed on a ratio scale (i.e. only using a MeasurementUnit) is computed as follows: \(v = f \cdot \log_b (x/x_{ref})^a \)
Context/Example (EN): LogarithmicScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a logarithmic measurement scale that is defined as follows. The numeric value v of a ratio quantity expressed on a logarithmic scale equivalent with a value x of the same quantity expressed on a ratio scale (i.e. only using a MeasurementUnit) is computed as follows: \(v = f \cdot \log_b (x/x_{ref})^a \)

Definition (EN): MeasurementScale is a MeasurementReference that represents a measurement scale.
Context/Example (EN): MeasurementScale is a MeasurementReference that represents a measurement scale.

Definition (EN): A MeasurementUnit is a ScalarMeasurementReference that represents a measurement unit. As defined in [VIM] a measurement unit is a "real scalar quantity, defined and adopted by convention, with which any other quantity of the same kind can be compared to express the ratio of the two quantities as a number".
Context/Example (EN): A MeasurementUnit is a ScalarMeasurementReference that represents a measurement unit.

Definition (EN): NullTransformation is an AffineTransformationMatrix3d that places the target CoordinateFrame at the same position and orientation as the source CoordinateFrame.
Context/Example (EN): NullTransformation is an AffineTransformationMatrix3d that places the target CoordinateFrame at the same position and orientation as the source CoordinateFrame.

Definition (EN): (No explicit description, but implied as a feature for NullTransformation)
Context/Example (EN): General Types: NullTransformation. Features: None.

Definition (EN): (No explicit description, but implied as a feature for DimensionOneUnit)
Context/Example (EN): General Types: DimensionOneUnit. Features: None.

Definition (EN): An OrdinalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents an ordinal measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only the ordering of quantity values is meaningful, not the intervals between any pair of values and neither their ratio.
Context/Example (EN): An OrdinalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents an ordinal measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only the ordering of quantity values is meaningful, not the intervals between any pair of values and neither their ratio.

Definition (EN): QuantityValueMapping is an AttributeDefinition that represents the mapping of equivalent quantity values expressed on two different measurement scales.
Context/Example (EN): QuantityValueMapping is an AttributeDefinition that represents the mapping of equivalent quantity values expressed on two different measurement scales.

Definition (EN): Representation of a rotation about an axis over an angle.
Context/Example (EN): Representation of a rotation about an axis over an angle.

Definition (EN): A ScalarMeasurementReference is a specialization of VectorMeasurementReference. It represents a single measurement reference for a ScalarQuantityValue or for a component of a tensor or vector quantity. Its order is zero.
Context/Example (EN): A ScalarMeasurementReference is a specialization of VectorMeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): SimpleUnit is a MeasurementUnit that does not depend on any other measurement unit.
Context/Example (EN): SimpleUnit is a MeasurementUnit that does not depend on any other measurement unit.

Definition (EN): A SystemOfUnits represents the essentials of [VIM] concept "system of units" (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.13.html), defined as a "set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities".
Context/Example (EN): A SystemOfUnits represents the essentials of [VIM] concept "system of units" (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.13.html), defined as a "set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities".

Definition (EN): A TensorMeasurementReference is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array, that represents the [VIM] concept measurement reference, but generalized for tensor, vector and scalar quantities.
Context/Example (EN): A TensorMeasurementReference is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array, that represents the [VIM] concept measurement reference, but generalized for tensor, vector and scalar quantities.

Definition (EN): Representation of a translation with respect to a coordinate frame.
Context/Example (EN): Representation of a translation with respect to a coordinate frame.

Definition (EN): TranslationOrRotation is an abstract union of Translation and Rotation
Context/Example (EN): TranslationOrRotation is an abstract union of Translation and Rotation

Definition (EN): A TranslationRotationSequence is a coordinate frame transformation specified by a sequence of translations and/or rotations.
Context/Example (EN): A TranslationRotationSequence is a coordinate frame transformation specified by a sequence of translations and/or rotations.

Definition (EN): UnitConversion is an AttributeDefinition that represents a linear conversion relationship between one measurement unit and another measurement unit, that acts as a reference.
Context/Example (EN): UnitConversion is an AttributeDefinition that represents a linear conversion relationship between one measurement unit and another measurement unit, that acts as a reference.

Definition (EN): UnitPowerFactor is an AttributeDefinition that represents a power factor of a MeasurementUnit and an exponent.
Context/Example (EN): UnitPowerFactor is an AttributeDefinition that represents a power factor of a MeasurementUnit and an exponent.

Definition (EN): UnitPrefix is an AttributeDefinition that represents a named multiple or sub-multiple measurement unit prefix as defined in ISO/IEC 80000-1.
Context/Example (EN): UnitPrefix is an AttributeDefinition that represents a named multiple or sub-multiple measurement unit prefix as defined in ISO/IEC 80000-1.

Definition (EN): A VectorMeasurementReference is a specialization of TensorMeasurementReference for vector quantities that are typed by a VectorQuantityValue. Its order is typically one, but can be zero in case of a specialization to ScalarMeasurementReference.
Context/Example (EN): A VectorMeasurementReference is a specialization of TensorMeasurementReference for vector quantities that are typed by a VectorQuantityValue.

Context/Example (EN): This package specifies concepts to support time-related quantities and metrology, beyond the quantities duration and time as defined in [ISO 80000-3].

Definition (EN): A Clock provides a currentTime as a TimeInstantValue that advances montonically over its lifetime.
Context/Example (EN): A Clock provides a currentTime as a TimeInstantValue that advances montonically over its lifetime.

Definition (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a calendar date.
Context/Example (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a calendar date.

Definition (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a calendar date and time of day.
Context/Example (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a calendar date and time of day.

Definition (EN): DurationOf returns the duration of a given Occurrence relative to a given Clock, which is equal to the TimeOf the end snapshot of the Occurrence minus the TimeOf
Context/Example (EN): DurationOf returns the duration of a given Occurrence relative to a given Clock, which is equal to the TimeOf the end snapshot of the Occurrence minus the TimeOf

Definition (EN): Representation of a time instant quantity. Also known as instant (of time) or point in time.
Context/Example (EN): Representation of a time instant quantity. Also known as instant (of time) or point in time.

Definition (EN): TimeOf returns a TimeInstantValue for a given Occurrence relative to a given Clock. This TimeInstantValue is the time of the start of the Occurrence, which is considered to be synchronized with the snapshot of the Clock with a currentTime equal to the returned timeInstant
Context/Example (EN): TimeOf returns a TimeInstantValue for a given Occurrence relative to a given Clock.

Definition (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a time of day.
Context/Example (EN): Generic representation of a time instant as a time of day.

Definition (EN): Generic time scale to express a time instant, including a textual definition of the meaning of zero time instant value. Attribute definitionalEpoch captures the specification of the time instant with value zero, also known as the (reference) epoch.
Context/Example (EN): Generic time scale to express a time instant, including a textual definition of the meaning of zero time instant value.

Definition (EN): universalClock is a single Clock that can be used as a default universal time reference.
Context/Example (EN): universalClock is a single Clock that can be used as a default universal time reference.
